rows.name: prompt
rows.description: The input text prompt for the model to generate a response.

rows.name: lora
rows.description: Name of the LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) model to fine-tune the base model.

rows.name: raw
rows.description: If true, a chat template is not applied and you must adhere to the specific model's expected formatting.

rows.name: stream
rows.description: If true, the response will be streamed back incrementally using SSE, Server Sent Events.

rows.name: max_tokens
rows.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

rows.name: temperature
rows.description: Controls the randomness of the output; higher values produce more random results.

rows.name: top_p
rows.description: Adjusts the creativity of the AI's responses by controlling how many possible words it considers. Lower values make outputs more predictable; higher values allow for more varied and creative responses.

rows.name: top_k
rows.description: Limits the AI to choose from the top 'k' most probable words. Lower values make responses more focused; higher values introduce more variety and potential surprises.

rows.name: seed
rows.description: Random seed for reproducibility of the generation.

rows.name: repetition_penalty
rows.description: Penalty for repeated tokens; higher values discourage repetition.

rows.name: frequency_penalty
rows.description: Decreases the likelihood of the model repeating the same lines verbatim.

rows.name: presence_penalty
rows.description: Increases the likelihood of the model introducing new topics.

rows.name: messages
rows.description: An array of message objects representing the conversation history.

children.name: role
children.description: The role of the message sender (e.g., 'user', 'assistant', 'system', 'tool').

children.name: Option 1
children.description: The content of the message as a string.

children.name: Option 2
children.description: Array of text content parts.

children.name: type
children.description: Type of the content (text)

children.name: text
children.description: Text content

rows.name: tools
rows.description: A list of tools available for the assistant to use.

children.name: name
children.description: The name of the tool. More descriptive the better.

children.name: description
children.description: A brief description of what the tool does.

children.name: parameters
children.description: Schema defining the parameters accepted by the tool.

children.name: type
children.description: The type of the parameters object (usually 'object').

children.name: required
children.description: List of required parameter names.

children.name: properties
children.description: Definitions of each parameter.

children.name: type
children.description: The data type of the parameter.

children.name: description
children.description: A description of the expected parameter.

children.name: type
children.description: Specifies the type of tool (e.g., 'function').

children.name: function
children.description: Details of the function tool.

children.name: name
children.description: The name of the function.

children.name: description
children.description: A brief description of what the function does.

children.name: parameters
children.description: Schema defining the parameters accepted by the function.

children.description: The type of the parameters object (usually 'object').

children.description: List of required parameter names.

children.name: properties
children.description: Definitions of each parameter.

children.name: description
children.description: A description of the expected parameter.

children.name: Option 1
children.description: Responses API Input messages. Refer to OpenAI Responses API docs to learn more about supported content types

children.name: Option 2
children.description: Responses API Input messages. Refer to OpenAI Responses API docs to learn more about supported content types

children.name: effort
children.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are low, medium, and high. Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response.

children.name: summary
children.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process. One of auto, concise, or detailed.
